evals.prompt: Generate the consensus transcript and provider comparison reports for the AutoShow STT run at runs/demo/.
evals.expected_output: The skill builds a consensus evidence packet, writes consensus-transcription.txt in [HH:MM:SS] [speaker-n] format, then regenerates reference-comparison-report.md and reference-comparison-report.json.
assertions: consensus-transcription.txt exists and every nonblank line matches [HH:MM:SS] [speaker-n] text format
reference-comparison-report.md exists and contains a ranking table
reference-comparison-report.json exists and is valid JSON
Speaker labels are stable canonical labels like speaker-1, speaker-2 throughout
The report provider count matches the number of providers/*/result.json directories
reference-comparison-report.md contains an Overall Ranking section with best overall and worst overall notes

reference-comparison-report.json has overallMetric, overallWeights, overall.count, tiering.groups.local, tiering.groups.thirdPartyDiarization, tiering.groups.thirdPartyNonDiarization, and provider overallComponents and supportsDiarization fields

evals.prompt: This run has an old comparison report that mentions providers that are no longer under providers/. Regenerate the reports from the current provider result.json files.
evals.expected_output: The provider set is derived from current providers/*/result.json files, stale report data is ignored, and the regenerated reports count only providers present on disk.
assertions: The regenerated report does not mention the removed provider by name in the ranking
The provider count in the report matches the number of result.json files on disk
The stale comparison report was not used as input
The regenerated report derives the overall provider count from current result.json files only

evals.prompt: Create a gold transcript from the STT run at runs/demo/. One provider has better technical terms but another has better speaker turns, so reconcile them carefully.
evals.expected_output: The consensus transcript preserves chronological segment lines and stable canonical speakers while repairing questionable text from cross-provider evidence without summarizing.
assertions: consensus-transcription.txt uses cross-provider evidence to select the best reading per segment
No segment text is summarized or paraphrased
Speaker labels are canonical and stable across the file
